answer:
Living in on-campus housing at Kennesaw State University (KSU) goes beyond just having a bed to sleep in; it's about forming relationships and finding your place within the community. Residents often find that living on campus helps them discover themselves, thanks to being around peers of similar age groups and attending various events happening on campus.

The university emphasizes building connections by encouraging students to interact with each other through shared activities or simply spending time together in their rooms/units. It's essential for residents not only to be aware but also proactive about safety measures, such as being alert and responsible within the community environment. They are advised never to allow strangers into their room or unit without proper verification of identity due to potential security risks.

Regarding guest policies at KSU, students can host guests in their rooms/units with prior consent from all roommates involved when hosting non-students and even other residential students who are not assigned as a roommate or roomie on campus. However, overnight guests must vacate the space after 3 AM to maintain respect for fellow residents' privacy and quiet hours. Cohabitation with someone outside of one’s designated housing assignment is strictly prohibited at KSU due to safety concerns associated with such arrangements.

For emergencies or non-emergency situations, students can contact the University Police directly via phone numbers provided on campus for immediate assistance without delay from 911 service which might be slower in response time when dealing specifically with university matters as it is a public system designed to handle various types of calls beyond just those related to KSU.

In summary, housing at Kennesaw State University offers more than shelter—it's an integral part of the student experience that fosters personal growth and community engagement while upholding safety standards for all residents through established policies and supportive staff presence around-the-clock.

sources.Title: Accepted Student Next Steps - Undergraduate Admissions
sources.Text:
an Out-of-State Waiver and Scholarship. Out-of-State Tuition Waiver Information 7) Join our ZeeMee Community A social platform for prospective and current college students, ZeeMee helps to connect you with peers, find a roommate, and learn about campus life! Join ZeeMee

1) Reply to your Admissions Offer (Accepting your Admission to KSU)

Let us know if you intend to come to KSU so we can plan your arrival to The Nest

2) File your FAFSA

Submit the FAFSA any time after it opens on October 1 . FAFSA is required to determine if you are eligible for any federal financial aid and to access loans.

3) Complete your profile on Scholarship Universe

KSU’s one-stop shop for available scholarships, Scholarship Universe, pairs you with potential scholarships that match the academic achievements and information outlined in your profile. KSU scholarships open annually on November 1 , and external scholarships are open throughout the year.

4) Apply for Housing

Applications for on-campus housing open on December 1 , and you are encouraged to apply early. On-campus housing is limited and assigned on a first-come, first-served basis.

5) Sign up for Mandatory New Student Orientation

Orientation allows you to connect with peers, complete class advising and course registration, and learn about resources. Early sign-up is encouraged for you and your parents/guests.

sources.Title: Housing and Residence Life
sources.Text: More Than a Place to Call Home Living on-campus at Kennesaw State University is about more than having a bed on campus—it's about building relationships and finding a community where you belong. Living on campus has helped me find myself, both by being in close proximity to other people my age and being able to attend events on campus. Key’Shawn Senior I chose to live on campus because I wanted to have an experience that would teach me something different from my small-town lifestyle. Living on campus has given me more opportunities to interact with other students on campus. Christopher Senior Living on campus, I always have a support system of people who are going through or struggling with the same classes I am. I also feel more connected to the campus. Alexis Sophomore

sources.Title: Residential Living - Housing and Residence Life
sources.Text: Residential Safety & Guest Policy BE SAFE All housing communities are protected by 24-hour video surveillance. Below are some precautions you can follow to help keep our communities safe for everyone: Be alert and take responsibility for the safety of your community. Do not allow strangers into your room/unit or community. Be sure to check that no one is following behind you (i.e. tailgating when entering buildings, propping open community doors, etc.). Always ensure your entryways are working effectively and notify RAs or CCs if you notice any failing equipment or security breaches. Report any suspicious persons or activities immediately, including those of your peers or roommates. GUEST POLICY When hosting a guest on campus, you must always accompany them. As the host, you are responsible for their behavior and may be held accountable for any misconduct. Residents may host guests of any gender expression in their room or unit with the consent of all roommates. Guests may include other KSU students, both residential and non-residential, or non-students. An overnight guest is defined as anyone who remains in the room or unit after 3 AM. Residents may have guests of any gender expression visit their room/unit, as long as the other residents sharing the space agree.

sources.Title: Residential Living - Housing and Residence Life
sources.Text: Guests may include other KSU students (both residential and non-residential) or people who are not students. An overnight guest is anyone who stays in the room/unit after 3 AM. Cohabitation, or staying with someone who isn’t assigned to your room/unit, is not allowed. of any kind is strictly prohibited. This includes the following behaviors/situations: Staying in a resident’s room/unit for more than three nights in one week (even if the nights aren’t in a row). Staying in a resident’s room frequently. Read more about the Guest Policy in the Student Code of Conduct (page 17). SAFETY AND SECURITY Download the LiveSafe app for information on keeping yourself safe on campus at KSU. Please call the University Police at 470-578-6666 for emergency situations. Dialing 911 will cause a delay in response time as 911 will receive the call and forward it on to the University Police. In non-emergency situations, you can still contact the University Police at 470-578-6206. Housing and Residence Life has para-professional and professional staff on-call 24 hours a day, 365 days a year. If you need help, please call your Resident Assistant. RA On-Call Phone Numbers
